frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: cached action-value learning that repeats reward-yielding actions without environmental model

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: planning via a probabilistic causal model of environment transitions and rewards

frameworks.name: causal Bayesian network
frameworks.one_line: latent-cause inference framework applied to hidden-state estimation in RL

core_question: What roles does causal knowledge play in reinforcement learning, and how do model-free and model-based systems coexist in the brain?
key_assumptions: model-free and model-based controllers operate as parallel learning systems
hidden states can be construed as latent causes generating sensory data and rewards
agents face a tradeoff between cheap-but-inaccurate and expensive-but-accurate computations
manipulations: review-style contrast of model-free vs model-based architectures
review of hidden state inference formalisms in RL settings
key_findings: evidence supports coexistence of model-free and model-based systems competing and cooperating in the brain
the brain negotiates a cost-accuracy balance between the two learning systems
hidden state inference impinges on both model-based and model-free RL via shared latent-cause representations
interpretations: causal knowledge is a unifying construct that links action selection with perceptual inference under uncertainty
latent-cause inference is a general mechanism shared across both RL controllers
